What is a ContextRefreshedEvent event?
options: This event is published when the Servlet Context is either initialized or refreshed.
This event is published when the HTTP Request is received.
This event is published when the HTTP Response is returned.
This event is published when the ApplicationContext is either initialized or refreshed.
correct: This event is published when the ApplicationContext is either initialized or refreshed.

question: What is a ContextStartedEvent event?
options: This event is published when the Servlet Context is either initialized or refreshed.
This event is published when the HTTP Request is received.
This event is published when the ApplicationContext is started using the start() method.
This event is published when the HTTP Response is returned.
correct: This event is published when the ApplicationContext is started using the start() method.

question: What is a ContextStoppedEvent event?
options: This event is published when the Servlet Context is either initialized or refreshed.
This event is published when the ApplicationContext is stopped using the stop() method.
This event is published when the HTTP Request is received.
This event is published when the HTTP Response is returned.
correct: This event is published when the ApplicationContext is stopped using the stop() method.

question: What is a ContextClosedEvent event?
options: This event is published when the Servlet Context is either initialized or refreshed.
This event is published when the HTTP Request is received.
This event is published when the HTTP Response is returned.
This event is published when the ApplicationContext is closed using the close() method.
correct: This event is published when the ApplicationContext is closed using the close() method.

question: What is RequestHandledEvent?
options: This event is published when the Servlet Context is either initialized or refreshed.
This event is published when the HTTP Request is received.
This event is published when the HTTP session is initialized or refreshed.
This event is published when the HTTP Request is serviced.
correct: This event is published when the HTTP Request is serviced.

question: In Spring AOP, what is an Introduction?

correct: A feature that allows you to add new methods or attributes to existing classes.

question: What is the primary role of the @Controller annotation?

question: What is the prototype scope in Spring?

question: What is session scope in Spring?

This scopes the bean definition to HTTP Application/ Global session.
correct: This scopes a bean definition to an HTTP session.

question: What is global-session scope in Spring?

question: What is true about the <map> collection configuration element?
options: This helps in wiring a list of values, allowing duplicates.
This helps in wiring a list of values but without any duplicates.

This can be used to inject a collection of name-value pairs where the name and value are both Strings.
correct: This can be used to inject a collection of name-value pairs where name and value can be of any type.

question: What is true about the <props> collection configuration element?
options: This helps in wiring a list of values, allowing duplicates.
This helps in wiring a list of values but without any duplicates.

This can be used to inject a collection of name-value pairs where the name and value are both Strings.

question: In Spring AOP, what is a Join point?
options: This represents a point in your application which joins two objects.
This represents a point in your object where you join values.
This represents a point in your object where you join injected values.
This represents a point in your application where you can plug-in the AOP aspect.
correct: This represents a point in your application where you can plug-in the AOP aspect.

question: In Spring AOP, what is Advice?
options: This is the way to instruct an object to behave in a certain manner.
This is used to inject values in objects.
This is the actual action to be taken either before or after the method execution.
This is not invoked during program execution by Spring AOP framework.
correct: This is the actual action to be taken either before or after the method execution.

question: In Spring AOP, what is a Pointcut?
options: This represents a point in your application where you can plug-in the AOP aspect.
This is a set of one or more join points where an advice should be executed.
This is used to inject values in objects.
This is invoked during program execution by Spring AOP framework.
correct: This is a set of one or more join points where an advice should be executed.

question: What is the Target object in AOP?
options: A represents an object in your application where you can plug-in AOP aspect.
The object being advised by one or more aspects, which will always be a proxy object.
This is used to inject values in objects.
This is not invoked during program execution by Spring AOP framework.
correct: The object being advised by one or more aspects, which will always be a proxy object.

question: What is Weaving in AOP?
options: Weaving is the process of injecting values in objects to create an advised object.
Weaving is the process of linking aspects with other application types or objects to create an advised object.
This is used to inject values in objects.
Weaving is used to check object dependencies.
correct: Weaving is the process of linking aspects with other application types or objects to create an advised object.

question: What are the ways to provide configuration metadata to Spring?
What is bean scope in Spring?
options: Bean scope forces Spring to produce a new bean instance as per the scope defined.
Bean scope defines the accessibility of bean in a java class.
Bean scope defines the accessibility of bean in a java package.
Bean scope defines the accessibility of bean in a web application.
correct: Bean scope forces Spring to produce a new bean instance as per the scope defined.

question: What is 'no' mode of autowiring?
options: Default setting which means no autowiring; explicit bean references must be used.
Spring first tries to wire by constructor, then byType.
Similar to byType, but applies to constructor arguments.
correct: Default setting which means no autowiring; explicit bean references must be used.

question: What is 'byName' mode of autowiring?
options: No autowiring; uses explicit bean reference.
Spring tries to match and wire properties with beans defined by the same names in the configuration file.
Spring first tries constructor autowiring, then byType.
Similar to byType, but applies to constructor arguments.
correct: Spring tries to match and wire properties with beans defined by the same names in the configuration file.

question: What is 'byType' mode of autowiring?
options: No autowiring; uses explicit bean reference.
Spring first tries constructor autowiring, then byType.
Spring matches and wires a property if its type matches exactly one bean in the configuration file.
correct: Spring matches and wires a property if its type matches exactly one bean in the configuration file.

question: What is the 'constructor' mode of autowiring?
options: Wires properties with the same names.
Wires property if type matches exactly one bean name.
Similar to byType, but applies to constructor arguments. A fatal error is raised if no unique bean is found.
correct: Similar to byType, but applies to constructor arguments. A fatal error is raised if no unique bean is found.

question: What is 'autodetect' mode of autowiring?
options: Spring first tries constructor autowiring; if it doesn't work, it tries autowiring byType.
Matches exactly one bean name in the configuration file.
correct: Spring first tries constructor autowiring; if it doesn't work, it tries autowiring byType.

question: Which is the correct implementation class of BeanFactory?
What does the @Required annotation mean?
options: The property must be populated by the user.
The property is required while saving data to the database.
The affected bean property must be populated at configuration time through explicit values or autowiring.
The property is required while serializing the bean.
correct: The affected bean property must be populated at configuration time through explicit values or autowiring.

question: What is true about the @Autowired annotation?
options: It can be used to autowire a bean on the setter method.
It provides more fine-grained control over where and how autowiring should be accomplished.
It can be used on methods with arbitrary names and multiple arguments.
correct: This annotation provides more fine-grained control over where and how autowiring should be accomplished.

question: In Spring AOP, what is an Aspect?
options: A way to perform dependency injection.
A module which has a set of APIs providing cross-cutting requirements.
A specific tool used only for logging information.
A class that represents the properties of a Spring application.
correct: A module which has a set of APIs providing cross-cutting requirements.

question: What is a Join point in Spring AOP?
options: A point in your application which joins two objects.
A point in your object where you join values.
A point in your object where you join injected values.
A point in your application where you can plug-in an AOP aspect.
correct: A point in your application where you can plug-in an AOP aspect.

question: What is a Pointcut in Spring AOP?
options: A point in your application where you can plug-in an AOP aspect.
A set of one or more join points where an advice should be executed.
A method used to inject values in objects.
A specific command invoked during program execution by the framework.
correct: A set of one or more join points where an advice should be executed.
